title: Chicken and mushroom pie
subtitle: Creamy chicken and mushroom filling under a shortcrust lid
excerpt: A 23 cm deep-dish chicken and mushroom pie: a rich sauce of chicken thighs, chestnut mushrooms, leek, and double cream in a shortcrust pastry lid. The filling is cooked on the hob first and cooled before the pastry lid goes on, so the pastry stays crisp rather than steaming from a hot fill. Serves 4.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: the chicken pie chapter documents a cooked and seasoned chicken filling finished with cream and placed under a puff or shortcrust pastry lid. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: Acton's covered meat pie section documents the technique of cooking the filling separately before assembling under a pastry lid, noting that a hot filling steams and softens the pastry. The recipe here follows the public-domain method of a cooked, cooled filling under a shortcrust lid.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze before baking: assemble the pie with cold filling and frozen lid, wrap, and freeze. Bake from frozen at 180°C fan for 50–55 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The filling keeps refrigerated for 2 days. Assemble and bake on the day.

recipeTools.notes: 23 cm deep pie dish or ovenproof casserole.

glossaryTerms.definition: The technique of allowing a cooked, sauced pie filling to cool to room temperature (or refrigerate it) before placing the pastry lid on and baking. A hot filling produces steam that condenses on the underside of the pastry lid, making it soggy before it has had time to crisp in the oven. A cold filling means the pastry bakes in dry oven heat, producing a crisp lid. The filling will heat through fully during the 25–30 minutes the pie bakes.

content.text: A chicken pie has one structural rule:

. A hot filling produces steam under the lid that softens the pastry from inside before the oven heat can crisp it. The filling is made on the hob first of chicken thighs, mushrooms, leek, and a cream sauce and left to cool completely before the pastry lid goes on. The bake is then purely about the pastry.

Rub the {{plain-flour}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{unsalted-butter}} cold butter together until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add {{water}} ice cold water a tablespoon at a time until the dough just holds. Press into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Melt {{unsalted-butter}} in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the {{leek}} and {{chestnut-mushrooms}} and cook for 5 minutes more until the mushrooms have released their liquid and it has mostly evaporated. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}} chicken pieces and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning, until sealed on the outside. Stir in the {{plain-flour}} and cook for 1 minute. Add {{chicken-stock}} gradually, stirring to prevent lumps. Add {{thyme-dried}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper-ground}}. Simmer for 15–20 minutes until the sauce is thick and the chicken is cooked through. Stir in {{double-cream}}. Taste and adjust seasoning. Spread on a plate and cool completely before assembling the pie.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Pour the cold filling into the pie dish. Roll the pastry to 4 mm thick and cut a circle 3 cm larger than the dish. Brush the rim of the dish with water. Drape the pastry over the filling and press the edges down firmly against the rim. Trim the excess. Brush the lid with {{eggs}} egg wash. Cut two steam vents in the centre. Bake for 25–30 minutes until the pastry is deep golden brown. Rest for 10 minutes before serving.
